mapping.a_element: machine-learning surrogate classifiers / Bayesian inference used to complement the dynamical analysis
mapping.b_element: bootstrap decision rule that infers from data whether a matched basis is worth building

brief_justification: The papers share no transferable machinery: paper A studies chaotic Hamiltonian orbital dynamics via chaos indicators (Lyapunov exponents, MEGNO, frequency map analysis) and anomalous-diffusion transport of trans-Neptunian orbits, whereas paper B computes an exact weighted-L2 projection error in a Wiener-Hermite polynomial basis whose Gram matrix gains off-diagonal terms under non-Gaussian excitation. Beyond a superficial, keyword-level appeal to statistical/ML inference in each, no governing equation, threshold, or process corresponds role-for-role, so they do not share an underlying method.
